findings.evidence: Roadmap Lane L says "Entirely finite combinatorics plus bilinear forms"; lines 62-74 call this a finite simple graph but define `structure PlumbingGraph (V : Type*)`, and lines 129-130 expose `IsNegativeDefinite := (-P.intersectionMatrix).PosDef` without `[Fintype V]`.
findings.fix: Make finiteness part of the public plumbing-graph API used for Lane L, or split/rename the general graph data and expose the finite plumbing-lattice API with `[Fintype V]` on the main definitions and predicates.
findings.issue: `PlumbingGraph` is documented and exposed as a finite plumbing graph, but the structure has no `Fintype V`/`Finite V` requirement; only the later form section adds `[Fintype V]`, while `intersectionMatrix` and `IsNegativeDefinite` remain public for infinite graphs.

summary: The new surface exposes plumbing graphs as if they are finite lattice data, but the core type and negative-definiteness predicate are available for arbitrary vertex types. Tighten the public API to the finite target or rename/document the generalized infinite version explicitly.
